references.title: Measurement of Self-regulated Learning: Strategies for Mapping Trace Data to Learning Processes and Downstream Analysis

extracted.Features: [Orientation, Planning, Monitoring, Evaluation, First-reading, Re-reading, Elaboration/Organization]
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Highighed Features are MicroSRL processes identified by the iauthors from the data. The table also includes common transitions.

Authors use a trace parcer to identify relevant transitions and identify sequences of actions that are fully connected from sequences where some of the expected actions are not fulfilled.
extracted.Cluster Description:
Using the Disconnected method, Monitoring seemed disconnected from all other SRL processes.

On the Connected perspective, all metacognitive processes seemed to occur simultaneously and ocuppying the “same space”.
extracted.Implications:
Trace parsing methods have a significant impact on the identification of SRL processes from log data. 

Disconnected methods may over-isolate some processes.
extracted.Comments: Authors perform a comparison of different types of trace parsing methods.
extracted.Limitations:
The effectiveness of Trace Parsing methods is yet to be fully discovered.

Requires more data and additional educational contexts.

annotations.text: Analysis of logs requires the transformation of the time-series-esque set of data into meaningful SRL constructs. The authors explore a different way of mapping interactions to SRL construct: Trace Parsing Methodology. They attempt to use 4 different forms of trace parsing and conclude that the choice of parsing method clearly affects process identification and sequencing.
